nativeRange.habitat: Marshes, riverbanks, and seasonally flooded wetlands along the Amazon basin and other tropical lowland river systems

summary: A classic large rosette sword plant from the Amazon basin; broad green lance-shaped leaves make it a striking background or solitary centerpiece for medium-to-large planted tanks.
careNotes: Echinodorus grisebachii is the workhorse 'Amazon Sword' of the aquarium hobby. Taxonomic note: based on 2008 DNA work, the species formerly traded as E. bleheri, E. bleherae, E. amazonicus, and E. paniculatus are now generally treated as synonyms of E. grisebachii (the wider trade complex is sometimes still split by hobbyists, but most plants sold as 'Amazon Sword' fall under this name). Adult plants are typically reported at 25–50 cm with arching lance-shaped leaves, occasionally exceeding 50 cm under strong light and rich substrate; figures up to 50 cm (Tropica) and 50+ cm (Aqua-Fish.net) are consistent across sources, so the upper end is averaged at 50 cm. Light demand is described as low by Tropica and moderate-to-high by most retail and care-guide sources; the average sits at medium and the plant tolerates a wide range, growing more slowly and laxly under low light. CO2 is not required (Tropica rates it 'low'; Modern Aquarium and Aquarium Co-Op note it is optional) but supplementation improves leaf size and density. This is a HEAVY ROOT FEEDER — multiple sources (Aquarium Co-Op, Modern Aquarium, Aquarium Breeder) emphasise that root tabs are essentially required and should be replenished monthly; a deep nutrient-rich substrate (~10 cm / 4 in) is recommended for a mature root mass. Temperature tolerance is broad: typical hobby range 22–28 °C, with reported tolerance from roughly 20–30 °C. pH 6.5–7.5 and gH 4–18 are well tolerated. Place as a solitary background or focal-point plant in medium-to-large tanks; in smaller tanks it quickly outgrows the layout. Newly purchased plants often arrive with emersed-form (broader, rounder) leaves that melt back as submerged leaves (longer, more lance-shaped) replace them — this is normal and not a sign of poor health.
breedingNotes: Propagates readily by adventitious plantlets ('daughter plants') that form along long emergent flower stalks; once each plantlet has a small rosette and visible roots it can be cut from the stalk and planted in the substrate. Mature plants also produce side runners and can be divided at the crown. Flowering and seed production are possible under high light and emersed/humid conditions but are uncommon in typical submerged aquarium setups.

primary.name: Tropica
primary.notes: Tropica page for Echinodorus grisebachii 'Bleherae' (the standard Amazon Sword form). Origin South America, height 20–50+ cm, light demand low, CO2 demand low, recommended as solitary plant for larger aquariums. Manifest-supplied search URL (https://tropica.com/en/search?q=Echinodorus%20grisebachii) returned only the site footer; navigated to the canonical plant-details page via Tropica's own search.

additional.name: Modern Aquarium
additional.notes: Secondary source. Confirmed temperature 20–28 °C (68–82 °F), moderate light preferred (tolerates low), CO2 optional, heavy root feeder, runners/side-shoot propagation, background/centerpiece placement, easy care level.

additional.name: Aquarium Co-Op
additional.notes: Cross-checked sizing (50+ cm / 20+ in), background placement, heavy root feeder needing 2–3 root tabs at planting plus monthly replenishment, easy care level.

additional.name: TFH Magazine — Echinodorus grisebachii
additional.notes: Used for taxonomy (family Alismataceae; 2008 DNA work consolidating E. amazonicus and E. bleherae under E. grisebachii), substrate depth recommendation (~10 cm), and propagation modes (crown division, plantlets on stalks, occasional flowering).

additional.name: Aqua-Fish.net — Echinodorus bleheri
additional.notes: Used to fill temperature range (20–30 °C), pH (6.5–7.5), and hardness (4–18 dGH) where Tropica/Modern Aquarium are silent. Propagation: adventitious plantlets on flower stalks plus daughter plants around the rosette.
